choices: Adds execute permission for the owner
items.explain: u+x adds the execute bit for the user (owner).

items.prompt: Why do we often run ./hello.sh instead of hello.sh?
choices: The current directory is usually not on PATH
Only root can run bare names
items.explain: Without ./ the shell searches PATH; . is usually omitted for safety.

items.prompt: umask sets default permission bits that are masked off when creating new files and directories.
items.explain: Bits set in umask are cleared from the creation mode.

choices: Owner read/write; group and others none
items.explain: 600 is rw------- — private to the owner.
